anti_cheat: DQ (score 0) if accounts.csv is altered vs the fixture (norm-compare, CRLF-safe) or deleted.
notes: Gauntlet Layer 1 (operator suggestedtests.md) — the strict-JSON / negative-constraint trap; tests OUTPUT DISCIPLINE, not raw capability. The agent must transform accounts.csv into benchmark-output.json: a MINIFIED single-line JSON object with the EXACT ordered top-level keys [schema_version,valid_accounts,rejected_rows,summary] and schema_version '1.0'. The discrimination lives in the fixture's trap rows (the input is fixed, so trap rows are the held-out signal): boolean variants for enabled (TRUE/true/1/yes vs FALSE/false/0/no/empty), an invalid-email row (no @) and a two-@ row, an invalid-plan ('premium') row, an empty-account_id row, and a DISABLED-but-also-invalid row that must be EXCLUDED entirely (in neither array, not in rejected_rows). row_number is 1-based among ALL data rows including disabled; reject precedence is account_id then email then plan from the fixed reason vocab. FORMAT (.4, partial): file exists, parses, no code fence, minified (no newline/double-space), exact ordered keys, exactly one new file beyond accounts.csv. CORRECTNESS (.6, partial): schema_version, ordered valid_accounts deep-equal, rejected_rows row_number set + reasons, summary counts, disabled rows in neither array. The oracle computes EXPECTED by parsing its OWN fixtures/accounts.csv with the same transform (never hardcoded). ANTI-CHEAT: altering accounts.csv => DQ score 0. validate.mjs proves: reference -> 1.0; code-fenced or pretty-printed -> format<1; dropped rejected row or disabled-row-into-valid_accounts -> correctness<1; an extra notes.txt -> format<1; tampered accounts.csv -> DQ 0.
